mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series over the prior quarter signals an easing policy path that lowers discount rates and lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is trending down for 3 months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than the prior 6-month pace, inflation surprise pressure forces tighter real-rate expectations and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses negative sign consistency after a confirmed CPI breakout.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE indicates improving labor demand and stronger household income expectations, which supports risk appetite and produces positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes fail to generate positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS signals weakening consumer spending, which leads earnings expectations lower and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS contraction episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if the signal’s short-horizon IC is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable or lower CPIAUCSL indicates productivity-led real activity rather than overheating, which supports margins and pushes SPX higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of higher INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL does not outperform baseline over 60 days, or if positive returns are not concentrated in the joint state.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals cooling housing demand and weaker downstream consumption, which tends to precede negative SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if two-month HOUST declines are not followed by below-zero 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening phase, a low and rising UNRATE signals deteriorating labor conditions that compress earnings expectations and produce negative SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes in a tightening cycle are not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the directional relation flips positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth decelerates while PAYEMS growth also weakens, the combined signal identifies demand deterioration that should depress SPX over the next quarter through lower earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-payroll slowdown state does not produce negative 60-day returns more frequently than either input alone, or if the conditional rank correlation is not negative.
